reasoning.research: HackerNews comment system has three distinct phases: (1) Draft - user types in textarea without submitting, (2) Submit - click submit button to post comment publicly, (3) Delete - click delete-confirm link to remove comment. Each phase is independent and reversible except deletion.
reasoning.strategy: Navigate to story comments page, draft a comment by clicking textarea and filling with text, submit it, verify it appears on page and comment count increases, then locate and click the delete-confirm link. The delete phase takes you to a confirmation page.
reasoning.llm_learnings: HackerNews requires 3 sequential steps for comment lifecycle: textarea → submit button → delete-confirm link. Comments are validated server-side (spam, killed by moderators). Always capture the comment ID for tracking. Delete links appear in the HTML row ABOVE the comment text, requiring careful HTML inspection.
reasoning.key_insight: The delete link format is 'delete-confirm?id=<comment_id>', not 'delete?id='. URLs use HTML entities (&) which must be decoded.
reasoning.verification_method: Count comments before/after to verify submission. Search for comment text in HTML to verify visibility. Check for delete link pattern specifically.

to_comment_form.description: Click textarea to focus comment form

draft_comment.description: Type comment text into textarea (draft state)

submit_comment.description: Click submit button to post comment

delete_comment.description: Click delete-confirm link (goes to confirmation page)

verify_posted.description: Verify comment text is visible on page

next_ai_instructions: This workflow demonstrates the complete HN comment lifecycle: draft → submit → delete. Critical insights: 1) Drafts are optional - you can submit without a draft state 2) Submitted comments appear immediately unless killed by spam filter 3) Delete links appear in the row metadata ABOVE the comment text, not below 4) Delete requires a confirmation step (goes to delete-confirm page) 5) Always verify comment count change to confirm submission 6) Use unique identifiers (timestamps) to track comments across sessions

html_parsing_notes.delete_link_location: Appears in metadata row ABOVE comment text, NOT inside comment div
html_parsing_notes.comment_count_position: Located in story header area, separate from comment section

safety_notes: Comments are publicly posted to HN - use test text that's clearly automated
Delete-confirm goes to a confirmation page - you must click confirm again to fully delete
HN spam filter may automatically kill comments from new accounts
Comments require minimum karma level to post
Rate limits apply - don't spam comment requests
Comments are part of your account history

metadata.notes: Workflow verified on live HN with authenticated session. Delete-confirm flow requires secondary confirmation page interaction.
